title: Aspidium haenkeanum
taxonomy: Aspidium haenkeanum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the kingdom Plantae. The genus Aspidium is now considered to be a synonym for the genus Dryopteris, making Aspidium haenkeanum a synonym for Dryopteris haenkeana. This fern is native to the Philippines and is known for its unique fronds. It is often found in tropical forests and is an important part of the local ecosystem.

intro: Aspidium haenkeanum, also known as the Haenke's lady fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical forests of the Philippines. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium haenkeanum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
